template_name: OMB M-24-10: Advancing Governance, Innovation, and Risk Management for Agency Use of AI

provisions.description: Federal agencies shall maintain an annual inventory of AI use cases, including purpose, deployment context, rights/safety impact classification, and responsible ownership, published publicly where appropriate

sub_provisions.description: Each AI use case must document the system purpose, decision influence level, and deployment context
Each AI use case must include a rights and safety impact classification with rationale

evaluation.message: At least one governance_policy record is required — OMB M-24-10 Section 5(b) mandates documented AI governance including use-case inventories

At least one governance_policy record with variant='ai_use_case_inventory_entry' is required — OMB M-24-10 mandates a structured AI use-case inventory with standardized fields for interoperability across federal agencies
AI use-case inventory evidence older than 1 year may not satisfy OMB M-24-10 annual reporting requirements
Governance policy records should be linked to a subject entity to identify which AI system the inventory entry pertains to

provisions.description: Each agency shall designate a Chief AI Officer (CAIO) responsible for coordinating AI governance, advising senior leadership on AI-related risks, and overseeing compliance with OMB guidance

evaluation.message: Governance policy records must include caio_designation documenting the Chief AI Officer per OMB M-24-10 Section 3 — CAIO is responsible for coordinating AI governance across the agency
At least one governance_policy record must have a non-null caio_designation — OMB M-24-10 requires every agency to name a Chief AI Officer

provisions.description: Agencies must classify each AI use case by its impact on rights and safety, distinguishing between rights-impacting, safety-impacting, both, or neither, with documented rationale

sub_provisions.description: Rights-impacting AI: AI whose output serves as a basis for a decision or action that has a legal, material, or similarly significant effect on an individual's or community's rights
Safety-impacting AI: AI whose output serves as a basis for a decision or action that could result in injury, damage, or harm to individuals, property, or the environment

evaluation.message: Governance policy records must include rights_safety_impact classification (rights_impacting, safety_impacting, both, neither) per OMB M-24-10 Section 5(c)
Governance policy records should include impact_classification_rationale documenting why the AI use case was classified at its rights/safety impact level

provisions.description: Agencies shall establish AI governance structures including governance boards, policies, and procedures to manage AI risks and ensure responsible AI use across the organization

evaluation.message: At least one governance_policy record is required — OMB M-24-10 Section 4 mandates an AI governance framework with policies, procedures, and organizational structures
AI governance framework governance_policy records should include attached policy documents (PDF or similar) demonstrating the established governance structure
